English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornelius W. de Kiewiet papers
De Kiewiet, C. W. (Cornelius William), 1902-1986.
3-7-372
1.6 cubic feet. 1.6 cubic feet.
1923-1950 Correspondence, photographs, manuscripts, research materials. Personal papers of de Kiewiet consisting of correspondence to and from de Kiewiet in South Africa, Rhodesia, London, Paris, Berlin, Iowa City, and Ithaca; also, lecture notes, records of Cornell University's Army Intensive Language Program, a diploma, photographs, and miscellaneous reflective and creative writings. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 15 - folder 1 ======= 1 15 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2093720
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Mikaela Hamilton
Date completed:
April 2017
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, April 2017
Date modified:
RMC Staff, April 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor, dean, provost, and acting president of Cornell University, 1941-51; subsequently president of the University of Rochester.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Personal papers of de Kiewiet consisting of correspondence to and from de Kiewiet in South Africa, Rhodesia, London, Paris, Berlin, Iowa City, and Ithaca; also, lecture notes, records of Cornell University's Army Intensive Language Program, a diploma, photographs, and miscellaneous reflective and creative writings.
Letter from C. W. de Kiewiet to Deane W. Malott, December 30, 1960, replying to a letter of sympathy on the death of de Kiewiet's daughter. de Kiewiet recalls Paul Tillich, while talking about the international conflict, saying that the great efforts that we are making to maintain ourselves are also the great efforts which may destroy us.
Cite As:
Cornelis Willem de Kiewiet Papers, #3-7-372. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Rhodesia -- Politics and government. South Africa -- History. Creative writing. Economic history -- Medieval, 500-1500. Malott, Deane W. (Deane Waldo), 1898-1996. Tillich, Paul, 1886-1965. Cornell University. Army Intensive Russian Language Program University of Rochester
